source_record.title: Antigen escape as a shared mechanism of resistance to BCMA-directed therapies in multiple myeloma.
source_record.study_context: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma treated with commercial teclistamab, including patients with prior BCMA-directed therapy exposure; PubMed abstract and PMC full-text study design/results sections used for this v0 public extraction.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports prior therapy-mediated loss of plasma-cell BCMA expression before teclistamab in three patients, with no observed response in those cases.
measurement_fields: anti-BCMA response after prior BCMA therapy

denominator_context.denominator_basis: Patients with pre-teclistamab plasma-cell BCMA expression available in bone marrow or plasmacytoma biopsy samples.
denominator_context.cohort_context: The source included 72 relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients treated with commercial teclistamab; 50 had pretreatment BCMA-expression assessment.
denominator_context.product_or_class_context: Subsequent teclistamab treatment; the three BCMA-expression-loss cases previously received non-teclistamab anti-BCMA-directed therapies, including belantamab mafodotin and anti-BCMA CAR T-cell therapy in the reported case narratives.
denominator_context.prior_bcma_exposure_context: The source reports 30 of 72 patients had prior BCMA-directed therapy exposure overall; the three BCMA-expression-loss cases had prior non-teclistamab anti-BCMA-directed therapy.
denominator_context.assay_method: Immunohistochemistry for BCMA and CD138 on formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded biopsy samples.
denominator_context.specimen_source: Bone marrow or plasmacytoma biopsy samples assessed for malignant plasma-cell BCMA expression.
denominator_context.target_assay_timing: Pretreatment assessment before teclistamab; the three expression-loss cases also had BCMA expression present before earlier non-teclistamab BCMA-directed therapy.
denominator_context.relapse_or_response_state: The three BCMA-expression-loss cases subsequently had no response to teclistamab; one also had no response to ciltacabtagene autoleucel.
denominator_context.paired_measurement_context: The article reports paired pre-initial-BCMA-therapy and pre-teclistamab biopsy contrast for the three BCMA-expression-loss cases.
denominator_context.frequency_claim_boundary: The 3 of 50 source-cohort target-assessment count may be preserved as denominator context, but it should not be generalized across BCMA-directed products, prior-exposure subgroups, relapse states, or all BCMA-directed therapy recipients without additional source-specific denominators.

limitations: Small mechanism-positive subset in one source cohort.
This extraction preserves a source-cohort target-assessment denominator but does not estimate frequency across all BCMA-directed products.
The prior-BCMA-exposed denominator within the target-assessed subset is not extracted as a separate denominator in this v0 record.
Treatment response interpretation requires full paper and clinical context.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports whole-exome sequencing evidence of biallelic TNFRSF17 loss in one tumor DNA sample after belantamab mafodotin exposure.
limitations: Single-patient genetic observation in the public abstract.
Does not prove the same alteration is common across relapse settings.
Needs full-text review before use in a claim set.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports low-to-undetectable peripheral blood soluble BCMA levels correlated with absent BCMA expression by bone marrow plasma cells.
limitations: Correlation is not sufficient for clinical decision-making by itself.
Assay thresholds and timing require full-methods extraction.
Public tooling should record soluble BCMA as a candidate measurement, not as a standalone rule.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source frames BCMA antigen verification as important before subsequent anti-BCMA treatment after prior BCMA-directed exposure.
limitations: This is not a treatment sequencing recommendation.
Applicability depends on therapy, disease state, assay availability, and clinician review.
The extraction only supports adding target-verification fields to public evidence maps.

what_this_supports: BCMA antigen loss or low expression should be tracked as a concrete post-BCMA relapse mechanism bucket.
TNFRSF17 alteration, immunohistochemistry target status, and soluble BCMA belong in extraction fields.
When a source reports target-status numerator and denominator fields, public records should preserve the source-cohort denominator basis before any frequency claim is considered.
Sequential BCMA-directed therapy records should preserve prior BCMA exposure and target-verification context.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not estimate antigen-loss frequency across all multiple myeloma relapse after BCMA-directed therapy; the 3 of 50 source-cohort target-assessment count is not a general rate.
It does not recommend teclistamab, CAR T, belantamab, or any treatment sequence.
It does not determine patient-specific eligibility or prognosis.
It does not establish that BCMA loss is the only shared resistance mechanism.

review_notes: Built from PubMed ESummary/EFetch metadata and PMC full-text study design/results sections for PMID 38728378.
Updated to preserve numerator, denominator, denominator basis, assay method, specimen source, timing, prior BCMA exposure, and response-state context for issue #4.
Mechanism statements should be converted into frequency claims only after additional source-specific denominators and expert review.
